latest.title: Subscription Agreement
latest.content:
Today 09:10
RNS Number : 5361U
World Chess PLC
26 February 2026
26 February 2026
World Chess Plc
("World Chess" or the "Company")
Subscription Agreement
World Chess Plc (LSE: CHSS), the London-listed chess company and official commercial partner of FIDE, is pleased to announce that it has entered a binding subscription arrangement with two of the Company's existing shareholders (together, the "Investors"), raising approximately £1,154,941 (the "Subscription").
Under the terms of the respective agreements, the Company will issue a total of 175,915,198 new ordinary shares of £0.0001 each (the "Subscription Shares") at an issue price of £0.00656533 per share (the "Issue Price"). The Subscription Shares will be allotted and issued fully paid and will rank pari passu with the Company's existing ordinary shares in all respects.
Completion of the Subscription is subject to the shareholder authority to issue shares to be proposed at a general meeting of the Company being held on 18 March 2026. Should such authority not be obtained, the number of shares to be issued to the Investors will be reduced to 127,605,998, being the current remaining share issuance authority.
As part of this transaction, the period for the fulfilment of the conditions of the warrants referred to in the Company's announcement dated 23 July 2025 will be extended by two years, from 31 December 2026 to 31 December 2028.
Ilya Merenzon, Chief Executive Officer of World Chess Plc, commented:
"This new capital allows us to fund our next phase of growth, and the continued commitment of our existing shareholders gives us both the resources and the confidence to deliver on it."
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended).
For more information, visit: www.worldchess.com or contact:
World Chess Plc
Ilya Merenzon, CEO
merenzon@worldchess.com
AlbR Capital Limited
Financial Adviser: David Coffman / Dan Harris
+44 (0) 20 7469 0930
Notes to Editors
World Chess (LSE: CHSS) is a London-based chess gaming and entertainment company and Fédération Internationale des Échecs ("FIDE") official commercial partner. World Chess organised the FIDE Championship Matches in the USA and the UK and revolutionised the sport by signing the biggest media partnerships in chess history. World Chess develops Armageddon, the chess league for prime-time television, and operates worldchess.com, the official FIDE-rated gaming platform. More at worldchess.com.

latest.title: Board Changes
latest.content:
12 Feb 2026 13:52
RNS Number : 8217S
World Chess PLC
12 February 2026
World Chess Plc
("World Chess" or the "Company" or the "Group")
Board Changes
World Chess (LSE: CHSS), a leading chess organisation that develops and operates worldchess.com, the official online gaming platform of the International Chess Federation (FIDE),
announces that Neil Rafferty has resigned from the Board of Directors, effective from 11 May 2026.
Mr Rafferty has given three months' notice in accordance with his contractual obligations and will continue to fulfil his duties as Interim Chair and Director until 11 May 2026, or until a new Chairman is appointed, whichever occurs first.
Statement by the Board
The Board would like to express its sincere gratitude to Neil for his valuable contribution and dedicated service to World Chess since the Company's IPO. Neil has played an important role during a significant period in the Company's development, and his insights and commitment have been greatly appreciated.The Board thanks Neil for his service and wish him every success in his future endeavours.
Chairman Succession
The Company has commenced a formal process to appoint a new Chair at the earliest opportunity.
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended).
For more information, visit:
www.worldchess.com

About World Chess Plc
World Chess (LSE: CHSS) is a leading chess organisation that develops and operates worldchess.com, a subscription platform with over one million users and the official online platform of the International Chess Federation (FIDE). The Company organized several World Chess Championships, including in New York and London. It organises the Armageddon Series and produces The World Chess Show, distributed across more than 50 international markets. More at worldchess.com.

latest.title: The World Chess Show Expands to BILD
latest.content:
23 Jan 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 0332Q
World Chess PLC
23 January 2026
23 January 2026
World Chess Plc
("World Chess" or the "Company")
The World Chess Show Expands to BILD
Key Highlights
World Chess plc (LSE: CHSS), a leading chess organisation that develops and operates worldchess.com, the official online gaming platform of the International Chess Federation (FIDE), today announces a significant partnership with BILD, Germany's largest media brand.
● The World Chess Show, one of the most widely televised chess properties in the world, will now air across BILD's premium sports media offers.
● With this addition, The World Chess Show now broadcasts across more than 156 markets globally, reinforcing its position as a leading chess television format.
● Content will be featured on BILD's digital platforms BILD.de & SPORTBILD.de within their 24/7 FAST-Channels (e.g. Best of BILD & SPORT BILD) reaching millions of German-speaking viewers.
● BILD reaches over 25 million unique users monthly, providing unprecedented exposure for chess content in Europe's largest economy.
Details of the Partnership
Under the media agreement, The World Chess Show will be broadcast across BILD's comprehensive sports media ecosystem, including:
● Digital Platforms - BILD.de and SPORT BILD, Germany's most visited news and sports websites
● Best of BILD FAST-Channel - Premium content showcase
● SPORT BILD FAST-Channel - non-stop sport highlights
About The World Chess Show
The World Chess Show is a magazine-style television programme designed to bring chess to mainstream audiences. Unlike traditional tournament coverage, the show is crafted for viewers who enjoy chess but do not follow competitive events closely - offering an accessible entry point into the world of professional chess through compelling storytelling, player profiles, and behind-the-scenes content.
The format firmly positions chess as a global sport, highlighting the drama, strategy and personalities that make it one of the world's most played games. By reaching audiences through mainstream sports channels, The World Chess Show is helping to redefine how chess is perceived and consumed by the general public.
Strategic Importance
The World Chess Show now airs across more than 156 markets internationally, establishing it as one of the most significant television properties in the chess world. This global distribution is central to World Chess's integrated media strategy: television exposure drives brand awareness and positions worldchess.com as the destination for viewers inspired to play. Each new market expands the potential user base for the Company's flagship digital platform.
BILD is Germany's most widely read and viewed news brand and one of Europe's most important media outlets. Germany represents a strategic market for World Chess, combining a strong chess tradition with Europe's largest population and economy.
Ilya Merenzon, Chief Executive Officer of World Chess, commented: "We built The World Chess Show to answer a simple question: why isn't chess on television the way football or tennis is? Chess has more than 600 million players worldwide - more than many other sports - yet it has been largely absent from mainstream sports broadcasting. We are changing that.
With BILD, we now reach over 156 markets globally. This is not just distribution for distribution's sake. Every viewer who discovers chess through The World Chess Show is a potential player on worldchess.com and a new member of the chess community. We are building something unprecedented: a media flywheel that grows our audience and our platform simultaneously.
Broadcasters are recognising that chess belongs in their sports programming, and we are the company making that happen. Germany, with BILD's extraordinary reach, is exactly the kind of market that accelerates everything we are building."
For more information, please visit https://company.worldchess.com/investors or contact:
World Chess Plc
Ilya Merenzon, CEO
merenzon@worldchess.com
AlbR Capital Limited
Financial Adviser: David Coffman / Dan Harris
+44 (0) 20 7469 0930
Notes to Editors
World Chess (LSE: CHSS) is a London-based chess gaming and entertainment company and Fédération Internationale des Échecs ("FIDE") official commercial partner. World Chess organised the FIDE Championship Matches in the USA and the UK and revolutionised the sport by signing the biggest media partnerships in chess history. World Chess develops Armageddon, the chess league for prime-time television, and operates worldchess.com, the official FIDE-rated gaming platform. The World Chess Show is a magazine-style chess programme airing across more than 50 international markets. More at worldchess.com.
BILD is Germany's largest-circulation newspaper and leading digital news platform. With over 25 million monthly unique visitors across its digital properties, BILD is one of Europe's most influential media brands.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: CHSS.L runs a global chess‑centric media platform. It produces live‑streamed tournaments, hosts online events, and sells branded merchandise and subscriptions to fans and players worldwide.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits at the intersection of sports entertainment and digital media. Chess has a massive, worldwide fan base and a growing esports presence. By offering live content and community tools, CHSS.L taps into a niche that is expanding as more people discover chess through streaming platforms.
company_30s.current_state: CHSS.L is in a heavy‑cash‑burn phase, raising capital through multiple equity placements and subscription deals. It has recently restructured its board, issued new shares, and secured partnerships with tech and sports organisations. The stock is trading at a very low level, far below the price it sold shares for a few years ago.

rns_narrative: The company is actively reshuffling its leadership and raising fresh capital via equity placements and subscription agreements. These moves aim to shore up cash, fund new streaming infrastructure, and expand its global reach through partnerships with chess federations and tech firms.

Two major themes emerge: (1) a drive to monetize through subscriptions and sponsorships, and (2) a strategic push to broaden its audience by collaborating with chess federations and blockchain platforms. The narrative suggests the firm is trying to pivot from a pure media house to a subscription‑powered ecosystem.

While the announcements signal a willingness to adapt, the repeated equity issuances hint at a fragile financial base that could erode shareholder value if the new cash does not translate into revenue.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Pessimistic silence – the community is largely quiet or negative, with very few bullish voices.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: None visible. The lack of bullish chatter is typical of a contrarian set‑up and signals that the market has not yet recognised any upside.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears argue the company is not profitable, keeps selling shares, and is losing board confidence. They fear further dilution and a failure to generate sustainable revenue streams.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Over the past few weeks sentiment has stayed flat or worsened, with no signs of optimism emerging. The chatter is dominated by concerns about board resignations and the company’s inability to monetize.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – there is no public interest in the ticker, which is a classic contrarian cue that a surprise event could be coming.

near_term_catalysts: The February 26 subscription agreement could inject fresh cash and signal a shift toward a recurring‑revenue model.
Any announcement of a new partnership with a major esports or blockchain platform could boost the company’s credibility and attract a wider audience.
A spike in trading volume around a board‑change or equity‑issuance announcement would confirm that investors are taking notice.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a price break above the recent low with a volume surge of at least 50 % over the 5‑day average. A positive earnings update or a clear revenue‑growth plan would also reinforce the bounce thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a deep‑crash bottom. With the APEX score (high) and dead sentiment, the mean‑reversion window is likely to open soon, especially if a subscription or partnership announcement occurs.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: Deep crashes of 70–95 % typically produce 30–40 % rebounds on average, with a win rate around 40 %. If the stock climbs from its current trough to a point halfway between the low and the pre‑crash price, the upside is roughly 100 %. The company’s new subscription model could act as a catalyst that pulls the price up faster than the historical mean, giving a higher return.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the equity placements continue without a clear revenue plan, the share price could stay depressed. A further equity dilution would push the price down again, and the company could face a liquidity crunch if it cannot monetize its content.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 100–150 % from the current trough. Potential downside: a further 20 % drop if the market rejects the new business model. The asymmetry favors the upside, making a cautious long position attractive.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the stock breaks the recent low with a 50 % volume spike. Size the position so that a 20 % move against you would trigger a stop 3–5 % below the entry. Use a trailing stop to lock in gains as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until a subscription or partnership announcement is made. Watch for a 10‑day moving‑average crossover or a 30‑day RSI rising above 40 as confirmation of a reversal.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – there are no immediate delisting or bankruptcy signals. The only terminal risk is continued dilution.

ai_insights.bottom_line: CHSS.L sits at a deep crash bottom with no market chatter; a subscription or partnership announcement could spark a mean‑reversion bounce, offering a high‑reward, low‑downside play for contrarian traders.
